name: André Lead Coverage Automation
description: The flagship automation. Watches the 9 blue diamond Smart Views in Close, covers every Andre-owned lead with a 10-day NEPQ-style SMS + email cadence via Close API, pauses immediately on any reply, classifies replies into 7 categories, schedules Slack DM reminders for call-time requests, and posts three daily reports plus a night safety sweep that compare against the prior snapshot. Every action runs through the EACE loop (Explore → Audit → Connect → Execute) and a post-processing EACE on the result. DRY_RUN and AUTOMATION_PAUSED are honored at every send. The automation NEVER places phone calls — every call is André's hands.
principles: EACE on every action. Explore → Audit → Connect → Execute. No phase skipped.
Post-processing EACE after every external action. An action without proof is no action.
Texts and emails ONLY via Close API. The system never calls. Never claims a call happened.
DRY_RUN=true is the default. Customer sends become [DRY-RUN] previews to the team channel.
AUTOMATION_PAUSED=true blocks all customer sends. Reports and observation continue.
Pause cadence on any reply. Resume is always manual.
9 blue diamond Smart Views are the operating surface. Nothing outside scope.
Triple anti-duplicate defense: ledger / Close activity / idempotency key.
Reports never modify ledgers. Reports are read-only observers.
Never auto-mark a lead lost. After Day 10 with no reply, surface for André review.
Owner change away from André = pause. Owner change to André = resume only if no manual review pending.
Three reports per day — morning, midday, night — each comparing against the prior snapshot.

batch_policy.notes: Small per-tick batches. The 5-min/10-min/15-min cadence ensures fresh visibility.

nodes.label: 🔷 01. Today's Leads
nodes.notes: Intake queue. New leads land here.
nodes.description: Lead Intake Sweep watches this view every 5 min. Each new lead becomes a fresh ledger + Day 1 send.

nodes.label: 🔷 02. Day 1-5 Cadence
nodes.notes: Active early-cadence cohort.
nodes.description: Cadence Sender pulls from here every 15 min for Days 1-5 of the playbook.

nodes.label: 🔷 03. Day 6-10 Cadence
nodes.notes: Active late-cadence cohort.
nodes.description: Cadence Sender uses the Day 6-10 messages from the playbook for leads in this view.

nodes.label: 🔷 04. Opened Email (24hr)
nodes.notes: Engagement signal — lead opened email recently.
nodes.description: An email-open is NOT a reply. Cadence continues. Lead is added to report's 'engagement signals'.

nodes.label: 🔷 05. No Connect Made
nodes.notes: Stale risk — no successful sends.
nodes.description: Reports flag any lead in this view with cadence_status: active but zero successful sends.

nodes.label: 🔷 06. Needs Response
nodes.notes: Manual review queue — the lead replied.
nodes.description: Any lead here MUST be cadence_status: paused. Mismatch = sync gap. Stuck >24h surfaces in morning report.

nodes.label: 🔷 07. Booked Tastings
nodes.notes: Progressing toward close — tasting on calendar.
nodes.description: Cadence STOPS. Status flips to completed_via_tasting. No more automated outreach.

nodes.label: 🔷 08. Long-Term / Dormant
nodes.notes: Nurture pile — NOT eligible for cadence.
nodes.description: Reports show count and sample. Cadence Sender never touches this view.

nodes.label: 🔷 10. All Other Followup
nodes.notes: Catch-all. Manual review only.
nodes.description: Surfaced in daily reports. Rapid growth >50%/day triggers warning — possible mis-routing.

nodes.label: .env config
nodes.notes: DRY_RUN, AUTOMATION_PAUSED, all credentials. Re-read every Explore phase.
nodes.description: .env carries the live kill-switch state and credentials. SKILL.md mandates re-reading at the start of EVERY Explore phase — the values are not cached because they're the panic-button surface.

nodes.label: diamond_views.json
nodes.notes: Maps view name → saved_search_id. Re-read on every session start.
nodes.description: Source of truth for which Close saved-search ID corresponds to each blue diamond view name. Re-read in case André renames or rebuilds a view.

nodes.label: state/leads/ ledgers
nodes.notes: One JSON file per lead under cadence. Append-only history.
nodes.description: The per-lead ledger. The single source of truth for cadence_day, cadence_status, last_sms_sent_at, last_email_sent_at, response_status, risk_flags, history. Atomic write via .tmp + rename.

nodes.label: state/snapshots/
nodes.notes: One snapshot per report run. Retain ≥14 days.
nodes.description: Report snapshots — totals, by-cadence-day counts, leads-in-view buckets, risk flags, call-time requests, comparison_to_previous. Reports diff against the prior snapshot to compute deltas.

nodes.label: state/errors/
nodes.notes: One file per day. Append-only. retryable: true|false per entry.
nodes.description: Daily error log. Each entry: timestamp, run_id, task, lead_id, action_attempted, error_type, error_message, retryable, next_retry_at. Permanent errors carry human_action_required.

nodes.label: state/runs/
nodes.notes: One run summary per scheduled-task fire.
nodes.description: Run summaries. Required for every session. Schema: task, run_id, started/ended, dry_run, leads_inspected, actions_executed/skipped/failed, ledger_files_updated, next_risks, errors. Reports compute deltas from these.

nodes.label: SKILL.md
nodes.notes: Operating skill. Read at start of every session.
nodes.description: The skill prompt every scheduled task reads first. Defines the EACE loop, kill switches, deferral rules, and what success looks like.

nodes.label: cadence-playbook.md
nodes.notes: Source of truth for the 10-day message copy.
nodes.description: The cadence playbook — Day 1 through Day 10 message templates. Cadence Sender reads this every fire and renders {{first_name}} / {{event_type}} substitutions.

nodes.label: response-classification.md
nodes.notes: 7-category classifier rules and priority order.
nodes.description: Response classification rules. 7 categories with explicit trigger phrases and priority order: not_interested → unclear_response → call_time_given → tasting_interest → pricing_question → send_info → needs_manual_review.

nodes.label: Lead Intake Sweep
nodes.notes: Schedule: */5 8-20 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #1. Find new André-owned leads in 🔷 01 and start cadence (or dry-run preview).

nodes.label: Ownership / View Sync
nodes.notes: Schedule: */10 8-20 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #2. Keep ledger state in sync with live blue diamond views and lead ownership.

nodes.label: Response Sync
nodes.notes: Schedule: */10 8-20 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #3. Detect inbound replies and pause cadences immediately.

nodes.label: Cadence Sender
nodes.notes: Schedule: */15 8-20 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #4. Send (or dry-run) any cadence messages whose send window is open right now.

nodes.label: Call-Time Reminder Creator
nodes.notes: Schedule: */15 8-20 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #5. For every call_time_given lead, ensure a Slack DM reminder is scheduled 5 min before the requested time.

nodes.label: Morning Report
nodes.notes: Schedule: 30 8 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #6. 8:30 AM ET. Compares against previous night's snapshot.

nodes.label: Midday Report
nodes.notes: Schedule: 0 13 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #7. 1:00 PM ET. Compares against the morning snapshot.

nodes.label: Night Report
nodes.notes: Schedule: 30 20 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #8. 8:30 PM ET. Compares against BOTH morning and midday.

nodes.label: Night Safety Sweep
nodes.notes: Schedule: 45 20 * * * ET
nodes.description: Scheduled task #9. 8:45 PM ET. Last line of defense — hunts for anomalies.

nodes.label: Explore
nodes.notes: Lead Intake Sweep — Explore: Gather facts. Take no action.
nodes.description: Lead Intake Sweep · Explore phase. Read views, ledgers, recent Close activity, last snapshot, kill-switches. NO writes, NO sends. (See eace-loop.md / post-processing.md.)

nodes.label: Audit
nodes.notes: Lead Intake Sweep — Audit: Pressure-test for safety + eligibility.
nodes.description: Lead Intake Sweep · Audit phase. Owner is André? Phone OR email? Already responded? Send already happened (anti-dupe)? Status terminal? DRY_RUN/AUTOMATION_PAUSED on? (See eace-loop.md / post-processing.md.)

nodes.label: Connect
nodes.notes: Lead Intake Sweep — Connect: Compare new facts to known state. Decide intent.
nodes.description: Lead Intake Sweep · Connect phase. Output a small specific list of intended actions, each tied to a lead and a reason. No execution yet. (See eace-loop.md / post-processing.md.)

nodes.label: Execute
nodes.notes: Lead Intake Sweep — Execute: Now do the work.
nodes.description: Lead Intake Sweep · Execute phase. Send via Close API, post Slack, write ledger, update Close note. Trigger post-processing immediately. Sign off with explicit status. (See eace-loop.md / post-processing.md.)

nodes.label: Post-EACE
nodes.notes: Lead Intake Sweep — Post-EACE: Second EACE on the result.
nodes.description: Lead Intake Sweep · Post-EACE phase. Did it actually happen? Did the API confirm? Was the ledger updated? Anti-duplicate cross-check. Sign off: success | dry_run_only | skipped | paused | failed | needs_manual_review. (See eace-loop.md / post-processing.md.)

nodes.label: Kill switch · DRY_RUN

config.behavior: post [DRY-RUN] preview to team channel; ledger=dry_run_only

nodes.notes: DRY_RUN=true: customers receive nothing. Previews go to the team Slack.
nodes.description: The first kill switch. When DRY_RUN=true, no customer-facing send happens — full message body posts to SLACK_TEAM_CHANNEL with a [DRY-RUN] prefix instead. Ledger writes use status `dry_run_only`. Reports continue normally.

nodes.label: Kill switch · AUTOMATION_PAUSED

config.behavior: block all customer sends; reports + response sync continue

nodes.notes: Panic-button switch. Stops sends without disabling schedules.
nodes.description: The panic button. AUTOMATION_PAUSED=true blocks all customer-facing sends regardless of DRY_RUN. Reports continue. Response Sync continues. Ownership Sync continues. Cadence Sender / Lead Intake / Reminder Creator skip Execute and log skipped: automation_paused.

nodes.label: Per-lead pause
nodes.notes: A single lead with cadence_status=paused/stopped/completed/failed receives no further sends.
nodes.description: Per-lead pause via the ledger's cadence_status. Reasons: lead replied, owner changed away from André, lead entered 🔷 06 or 🔷 07, two consecutive send failures, manual hold. Resume is always manual — never auto.

nodes.label: Anti-dupe · ledger check
nodes.notes: Layer 1: re-read ledger. SMS within 6h or email within 12h → skip.
nodes.description: Layer 1 anti-duplicate defense. Before any send, re-read the lead's ledger fresh from disk. Check cadence_day, last_sms_sent_at, last_email_sent_at, and the most-recent history entry. If any signal says already-sent, skip.

nodes.label: Anti-dupe · Close activity
nodes.notes: Layer 2: query Close. Outbound on same channel in last 4h → skip.
nodes.description: Layer 2 anti-duplicate defense. Even if the ledger says nothing was sent, query Close itself for recent outbound SMS/email activities on this lead. Catches manual sends by André or another rep.

nodes.label: Anti-dupe · idempotency key
nodes.notes: Layer 3: today's run summaries. Same key already done → skip.
nodes.description: Layer 3 anti-duplicate defense. Every cadence-send action carries an idempotency key. If today's run summaries already contain that key, the send is skipped.

nodes.label: Day 1 · NEPQ open
nodes.notes: Window: 1-5 min after André-owned. Channels: sms, email.
nodes.description: Cadence Day 1. 1-5 min after André-owned. Renders the message from cadence-playbook.md, substitutes {first_name} / {event_type}. If the lead replies, this day is skipped.

nodes.label: Day 1 +3-4h · still comparing?
nodes.notes: Window: +3-4h if no reply. Channels: sms, email.
nodes.description: Cadence Day 1_2n. +3-4h if no reply. Renders the message from cadence-playbook.md, substitutes {first_name} / {event_type}. If the lead replies, this day is skipped.

nodes.label: Day 2 · pricing vs tasting
nodes.notes: Window: 9:30-11:30 AM ET. Channels: sms, email.
nodes.description: Cadence Day 2. 9:30-11:30 AM ET. Renders the message from cadence-playbook.md, substitutes {first_name} / {event_type}. If the lead replies, this day is skipped.

nodes.label: Day 3 · phone call ask

nodes.description: Cadence Day 6. 2:00-4:30 PM ET. Renders the message from cadence-playbook.md, substitutes {first_name} / {event_type}. If the lead replies, this day is skipped.

nodes.label: Day 9 · what's in the way?
nodes.notes: Window: 3:00-5:30 PM ET. Channels: sms, email.
nodes.description: Cadence Day 9. 3:00-5:30 PM ET. Renders the message from cadence-playbook.md, substitutes {first_name} / {event_type}. If the lead replies, this day is skipped.

nodes.label: Day 10 · stepping back
nodes.notes: Window: 10:00 AM-12:00 PM ET. Channels: sms, email.
nodes.description: Cadence Day 10. 10:00 AM-12:00 PM ET. Renders the message from cadence-playbook.md, substitutes {first_name} / {event_type}. If the lead replies, this day is skipped.

nodes.label: Cadence completed
nodes.notes: Day 10 done with no reply. Status flips to `completed`. Never auto-marks lost.
nodes.description: Reached after Day 10's send completes with no response received. Sets cadence_status: completed. Adds Close note: '10-day text/email cadence completed with no response. Do not mark lost automatically. André review recommended.' Lead is added to next morning report's 'Cadence completed — needs review' bucket.

nodes.label: Pause cadence (any reply)
nodes.notes: The first thing every reply does. Pause is universal.
nodes.description: Universal first step on any inbound reply. Sets cadence_status: paused (or stopped_not_interested). Writes a Close note. Posts an alert to SLACK_ANDRE_DM_CHANNEL. Then the classifier branches by response type.

nodes.label: Classify reply (priority order)
nodes.notes: First match wins. Multi-intent → highest priority bucket; André's manual reply covers the rest.
nodes.description: Run categories in priority order. not_interested ALWAYS wins (must honor opt-outs). unclear_response checked second (empty/MMS-only/malformed bodies must surface as ambiguity, not be guessed at). Then the normal categories. needs_manual_review is the catch-all.

nodes.label: Not interested

config.action: stop cadence, optional polite closer, Slack DM alert. Do not auto-mark lost.

nodes.notes: stop cadence, optional polite closer, Slack DM alert. Do not auto-mark lost.
nodes.description: Response classification: Not interested. stop cadence, optional polite closer, Slack DM alert. Do not auto-mark lost.

nodes.label: Unclear / no parseable text

config.action: pause cadence, NO auto-reply, post alert with whatever metadata available.

nodes.notes: pause cadence, NO auto-reply, post alert with whatever metadata available.
nodes.description: Response classification: Unclear / no parseable text. pause cadence, NO auto-reply, post alert with whatever metadata available.

nodes.label: Call time given

config.action: pause, parse time, schedule Slack reminder T-5min, send confirmation SMS, Close note, DM alert.

nodes.notes: pause, parse time, schedule Slack reminder T-5min, send confirmation SMS, Close note, DM alert.
nodes.description: Response classification: Call time given. pause, parse time, schedule Slack reminder T-5min, send confirmation SMS, Close note, DM alert.

nodes.label: Tasting interest

config.action: pause, send tasting-quick-call reply, alert André.

nodes.notes: pause, send tasting-quick-call reply, alert André.
nodes.description: Response classification: Tasting interest. pause, send tasting-quick-call reply, alert André.

nodes.label: Pricing question

config.action: pause, send 'pricing depends on guest count + style + menu' reply asking guest count, alert André. Never auto-quote.

nodes.notes: pause, send 'pricing depends on guest count + style + menu' reply asking guest count, alert André. Never auto-quote.
nodes.description: Response classification: Pricing question. pause, send 'pricing depends on guest count + style + menu' reply asking guest count, alert André. Never auto-quote.

nodes.label: Send info

config.action: pause, send brief reply asking event type, alert André.

nodes.notes: pause, send brief reply asking event type, alert André.
nodes.description: Response classification: Send info. pause, send brief reply asking event type, alert André.

nodes.label: Needs manual review

config.action: pause, NO auto-reply, ping André's DM with reply text + Close link.

nodes.notes: pause, NO auto-reply, ping André's DM with reply text + Close link.
nodes.description: Response classification: Needs manual review. pause, NO auto-reply, ping André's DM with reply text + Close link.

nodes.label: Risk flag bank (9 flags)
nodes.notes: Canonical risk-flag list. Each ledger carries an array of active flags. Reports surface them.
nodes.description: The 9 canonical risk flags from safety-rules.md. Active flags surface in every report's Risk List. Flags get added by Audit (no_contact_info, urgent_event_date, ownership_conflict), Connect (cadence_near_end, tasting_interest_unactioned), and Post-EACE (send_failed, needs_andre_review).

nodes.label: Close API · SMS activity
nodes.notes: All cadence/response SMS go through Close API as activities. Phone ID required.
nodes.description: Close API SMS activity sink. Every outbound SMS is created here as a Close activity using the group line +19783811212. In DRY_RUN mode, the would-send body is posted to SLACK_TEAM_CHANNEL with a [DRY-RUN] prefix and the Close API is NOT called.

nodes.label: Close API · Email activity
nodes.notes: All cadence/response email go through Close as activities.
nodes.description: Close API email activity sink. Outbound email from team@comeketocatering.com so replies land in the shared inbox. DRY_RUN posts to team channel instead.

nodes.label: Close API · Note activity
nodes.notes: Every notable workflow event writes one Close note for the timeline.
nodes.description: Close note sink. Cadence start, every send, every reply, every pause, every reminder creation, cadence completion — all write structured notes here so the Close timeline tells the full story.

nodes.label: Slack DM · André
nodes.notes: Realtime alerts to André's DM. Replies, call-times, send failures, ownership conflicts.
nodes.description: André's Slack DM (D0AMX3BV64T) — the realtime visibility layer. Replies, call-times, tasting interest, pricing questions, send failures, ownership conflicts, safety-sweep findings.

nodes.label: Slack channel · team
nodes.notes: Team channel — daily reports, dry-run previews, baseline announcements.
nodes.description: Team Slack channel (C0AV913L1LJ). Receives the three daily reports (morning/midday/night), dry-run send previews, baseline-snapshot announcements, and any team-wide visibility posts.

nodes.label: Slack · scheduled message
nodes.notes: Scheduled future Slack post — used for the 5-min-before-call reminder.
nodes.description: Slack scheduled-message sink. Used by Call-Time Reminder Creator to post to D0AMX3BV64T at requested_time minus 5 minutes with: 📞 Call {lead_name} · phone · Close link · original ask.

connections.description: Lead Intake Sweep Execute queues Day 1 SMS + email immediately (within 1-5 min of becoming André-owned).
connections.label: queue Day 1

connections.description: After every send (or dry-run), the ledger is updated atomically with a new history entry and updated last_*_sent_at.
connections.label: append history

connections.description: Response Sync Execute pauses the cadence (cadence_status: paused) and appends a response_received history entry.
connections.label: pause + history

connections.description: Ownership Sync Execute reconciles the ledger with the live Close + view state. Pauses if owner left André.
connections.label: sync state

connections.description: If ownership flipped away from André, post an alert to his DM.
connections.label: conflict

connections.description: intake Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: ownersync Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: respsync Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: cadence Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: calltime Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: morning Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: midday Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: night Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: safety Post-EACE writes the required run summary to state/runs/.
connections.label: run summary

connections.description: Cadence Post-EACE logs send failures with retryable + next_retry_at to today's error file.
connections.label: if failed

connections.description: If both SMS and email fail, alert André's DM. The cadence is marked failed.
connections.label: both fail
